title: Actinostemon cuneatus
taxonomy: Actinostemon cuneatus is classified within the family Phyllanthaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the Phyllanthus family. This species belongs to the genus Actinostemon, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The classification of Actinostemon cuneatus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species is also known by its synonym, Actinostemon cuneatus is not known by any synonyms, it is also classified as a species under the phylum Magnoliophyta. The full classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Malpighiales, family Phyllanthaceae, and genus Actinostemon.

intro: Actinostemon cuneatus is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, commonly known as "cunha" or "miraí" in Portuguese, which is a type of shrub or small tree that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Actinostemon cuneatus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for wound care and skin conditions.
